body:
This example includes no explicit modification to the BCs on buoyancy, meaning that it defaults to a no-flux BC (or `FluxBoundaryCondition()`) on buoyancy.

Following equation (6) of [Wenegrat and Thomas (2020)](https://journals.ametsoc.org/view/journals/phoc/50/6/JPO-D-20-0027.1.xml), we instead enforce a no-normal diffusive flux boundary condition on the *total* buoyancy, i.e. the perturbation plus the constantly-stratified `BackgroundField`. Because Oceananigans.jl does not allow diffusion to act on `BackgroundField`s, the background part of the no-flux BC is missing. However, we here can enforce it by specifying a perturbation flux BC that matches the implied background flux.

Other minor changes:
- Added equally-spaced buoyancy surfaces to movie panels
- Guess timestep by minimum of advective and diffusive timescales, instead of just the advective timescale

This addresses the problem with the `tilted_bottom_boundary_layer.jl` example identified by @liuchihl and noted in https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/issues/3568. Recommend closing that issue but perhaps continuing some of the discussion in a "Discussion" post.
title: Enforce total buoyancy flux BC in tilted geometry example
